content.text: Answer seven problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

body.text: Let \(K\) be a field and let \(f(x) \in K[x]\) be a non-zero polynomial.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what is a splitting field for \(f(x)\) over \(K\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: If \(F_1\) and \(F_2\) are splitting fields for \(f(x)\) over \(K\) prove, from your definition, that \(F_1\) and \(F_2\) are isomorphic fields.

body.text: Let \(F=\mathbb{Q}(\sqrt{3},\sqrt{5})\) be the smallest subfield of \(\mathbb{C}\) which contains \(\sqrt{3}\) and \(\sqrt{5}\). How many subfields does \(F\) have?
Let \(A\) and \(B\) be finite abelian groups and suppose that \(|A|\) and \(|B|\) are relatively prime. Let \(T=A\otimes_{\mathbb{Z}}B\). Show that \(|T|=1\).
Let \(C\) be a category and let \((A_i)_{i\in I}\) be a family of objects of \(C\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what is a coproduct of \((A_i)_{i\in I}\) in \(C\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that any two coproducts of \((A_i)_{i\in I}\) in \(C\) are equivalent (some texts would say isomorphic) in \(C\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what it means for an \(R\)-module \(M\) to be projective.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text:
Prove that if
\[0\to A\to B\to P\to 0\]
is an exact sequence of \(R\)-modules and \(P\) is projective then the exact sequence splits.

body.text: Let \(F\) be a free group with free generators \(a\) and \(b\). Let \(N\) be the smallest normal subgroup of \(F\) such that \(a^2\in N\) and \(b^2\in N\). Prove that \(F/N\) is infinite.
State and prove the Lying-Over Theorem on prime ideals and ring extensions.

Prove the following version of Nakayama’s Lemma. Let \(A\) be a commutative ring with identity, let \(M\) be a finitely generated \(A\)-module, and let \(I\) be an ideal contained in the intersection of all the maximal ideals of \(A\). Show that if \(IM=M\) then \(M=\{0\}\).
An invertible ideal in an integral domain that is a local ring is principal.

Let \(\overline{\mathbb{Q}}\) be an algebraic closure of \(\mathbb{Q}\) and \(v\in\overline{\mathbb{Q}}\) with \(v\notin\mathbb{Q}\). Let \(E\) be a subfield of \(\overline{\mathbb{Q}}\) maximal with respect to the condition that \(v\notin E\). Prove that every finite-dimensional extension of \(E\) is cyclic.
Classify (up to ring isomorphism) all semisimple rings of order \(720\).
